paragraphs.en: This is my room. It is small but I like it. The walls are white.
paragraphs.fr: Voici ma chambre. Elle est petite mais je l'aime bien. Les murs sont blancs.

paragraphs.en: There is a bed near the window. Next to the bed, there is a small desk. I do my homework at the desk.
paragraphs.fr: Il y a un lit pres de la fenetre. A cote du lit, il y a un petit bureau. Je fais mes devoirs au bureau.

paragraphs.en: There is a lamp on the desk. The lamp is blue. There are two books on the desk too.
paragraphs.fr: Il y a une lampe sur le bureau. La lampe est bleue. Il y a aussi deux livres sur le bureau.

paragraphs.en: I love my room. It is my favourite place in the house.
paragraphs.fr: J'adore ma chambre. C'est mon endroit prefere dans la maison.

vocabulary.definition_en: une table utilisee pour ecrire ou travailler
vocabulary.example: My computer is on the desk.

vocabulary.definition_en: une ouverture dans un mur avec du verre pour laisser entrer la lumiere
vocabulary.example: I can see the garden from my window.

vocabulary.definition_en: le cote d'une piece ou d'un batiment
un objet qui donne de la lumiere
vocabulary.example: I turn on the lamp at night.
My room is small but nice.

grammar_points.title: "There is" / "There are"
grammar_points.explanation: Utilisez **"There is"** (il y a) devant **une seule chose** (singulier). Utilisez **"There are"** (il y a) devant **deux choses ou plus** (pluriel). En français, 'il y a' ne change pas, mais en anglais, si !
examples: There is a bed in my room. = Il y a un lit dans ma chambre. (one bed)
There is a lamp on the desk. = Il y a une lampe sur le bureau. (one lamp)
There are two books on the desk. = Il y a deux livres sur le bureau. (two books)
There are three windows. = Il y a trois fenetres. (three windows)

expressions.example: In my room, there is a big bed and a small desk.
expressions.note: Une façon simple de commencer à décrire ce qu'il y a dans un endroit.

expressions.example: The lamp is next to the bed.
expressions.note: S'utilise pour dire où se trouve quelque chose. Autres mots utiles : 'on' (sur), 'near' (près de), 'under' (sous).

written.question: Which sentence is correct?
options: There is two books on the desk.
There are two books on the desk.
There are a book on the desk.
written.explanation: We use 'There are' before plural nouns (two books). 'There is' is for singular nouns.

written.question: What does 'next to' mean in French?
written.explanation: 'Next to' means 'a cote de'. 'Sous' = under, 'sur' = on.

written.sentence: There ________ a lamp on the desk.
written.hint: 'There is' for one thing, 'there are' for many things.

written.french: Il y a un lit et un bureau dans ma chambre.
written.suggested_answer: There is a bed and a desk in my room.

oral.instruction: Read these sentences aloud slowly and clearly.
oral.text: In my room, there is a bed and a desk. The lamp is next to the window.
oral.focus: Practice the 'th' sound in 'there' and 'the'. Speak slowly and clearly.

oral.question: What is in your room? Describe two or three things.
oral.suggested_answer: In my room, there is a bed. There is a desk next to the window. There are books on the desk.
